title: US and Iran intensify mutual attacks; Iran threatens to block Strait of Hormuz
summary: The escalation of war raises market uncertainty, with oil rising and Asian stock markets falling.

title: Japan's Supreme Court recognizes residents' standing to sue over Henoko base relocation
summary: The historic decision allows citizens to continue litigation against the relocation of the US military base in Okinawa.

title: Four people stabbed in Oita; 44-year-old man arrested
summary: The attacker, who randomly attacked in a parking lot and on the street, stated that anyone was fine as a victim.

title: Kabuki actor Nakamura Kosaburo investigated for alleged hit-and-run
summary: Metropolitan police conduct searches and gather testimonies about the incident involving the famous performer.

title: Supplementary budget bill postponed in House of Representatives; government seeks to extend session
summary: The prime minister urges the ruling party to pass the bill during the current period, amid controversies.

title: Imperial Household Law reform enters debate in Upper House committee
summary: The opposition party demands modifications to remove the restriction of succession only to males of the male line.

title: Fire at Bangkok bar kills 28; majority in the venue's bathroom
summary: The victims were trapped inside and no Japanese citizens are reported among the deceased.

title: Government and opposition agree to introduce refundable tax credit for low-income workers
summary: The measure aims to alleviate the tax burden, although it raises concerns about the administrative burden for municipalities.

title: EU proposes restricting social media use for children; Japanese experts endorse the measure
summary: The new rules would require parental supervision for children under 13 and prohibit access for children under 3.

title: Extreme heat wave in Japan; heatstroke alerts triggered in 14 prefectures
summary: Temperatures exceed 38 degrees in some regions, with high risk of heatstroke even in eastern Japan.

title: Zelensky announces cabinet changes and Ukrainian forces intensify attacks in the Sea of Azov
summary: The prime minister would be reassigned as ambassador to the US, while bombings against Russian infrastructure continue.

title: Disaster Management Agency created in Japan, to start operations in November
summary: The new entity will centralize government response to emergencies and will have regional offices.

title: Cyberattack against Nichirei causes disruptions in frozen food shipments
summary: The company reports unauthorized access affecting its system, with no confirmed leak of personal data.

title: Actor Sam Neill, known for his role in 'Jurassic Park', dies at 78
summary: The New Zealander performer leaves a legacy in film and television; his death was confirmed by close sources.

title: Syrian parliament meets for first time after fall of Assad regime
summary: The inaugural session seeks to integrate minorities and women in the transitional political process.

title: Decision not to indict NHK party leader for threats against lawmaker reviewed
summary: The prosecution review committee considers the initial decision inappropriate and orders a new investigation.

title: Aeroflot's Tokyo office reportedly operated as Russian spy base
summary: Undercover Russian military officers acquired cutting-edge technology in Japan, according to international reports.

title: Turkish citizen dies in police custody at Takao police station
summary: Police investigate the circumstances of the death, which is presumed to be from natural causes.

title: LDP proposes measures to increase female representation in Parliament
summary: Suggestions include prioritizing women on proportional representation lists for the next elections.

title: Man arrested for homicide after hit-and-run in Kyoto
summary: The accident, which involved three vehicles, caused the death of a woman.
